lvl-up-expo-2026-1.name: LVL UP EXPO 2026
lvl-up-expo-2026-1.location: 3150 Paradise Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89109, USA
lvl-up-expo-2026-1.description:
LVL UP EXPO 2026 returns to Las Vegas for a massive three-day celebration of gaming, anime, and competitive esports. Scheduled for April 24 through April 26, 2026, the event will take place at the Las Vegas Convention Center in the South Hall, bringing together thousands of global competitors and fans.

As a major destination for the fighting game community, the 2026 tournament features a heavyweight lineup. The main stages will host massive brackets for Street Fighter 6, Super Smash Bros. Ultimate, and Tekken 8, ensuring the highest-stakes matches are witnessed by a capacity crowd.

Elevated Stakes and Prize Pools

The 2026 iteration boasts over $40,000 in combined prize pools across its flagship tournaments. Highlights include a $10,000 prize pool for Smash Ultimate Singles, $10,000 for 2XKO, and $5,000 each for Street Fighter 6 and Tekken 8, raising the stakes significantly for the global talent in attendance.

Modern Heavyweights and Legacy Games

This year's iteration features established staples of the current competitive circuit. Attendees will compete in Riot's blockbuster team-based fighter 2XKO and battle it out in SNK's acclaimed Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves. Additionally, Hunter x Hunter: Nen x Impact makes its appearance alongside other modern anime fighters.

Honoring the community's rich history, the lineup also includes the LVStylo Memorial Tournament for Ultra Street Fighter IV, alongside returning favorites like Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising, Guilty Gear: Strive, Under Night In-Birth II Sys:Celes, Mortal Kombat 1, and Ultimate Marvel vs Capcom 3.

Registration

Registration and bracket sign-ups are managed through Start.gg and the official LVL UP EXPO website. Competitors can secure their spots in the brackets until April 21, 2026, with general admission passes available for spectators wishing to experience the event floor, artist alley, and vendor exhibits.

evo-japan-2026.name: Evo Japan 2026
evo-japan-2026.description:
Evo Japan 2026 marks the return of the world's most prestigious fighting game brand to Tokyo. Scheduled for May 1 through May 3, 2026, the event will take place at the iconic Tokyo Big Sight, utilizing East Halls 1-3 to accommodate thousands of global competitors and spectators.

As the first Evo event of the 2026 season, the tournament features a massive 12-game lineup. In a major shift for the series, the Arena Finals (Main Stage) will be determined entirely by player support: the six games with the highest registration numbers will secure the coveted main stage slots.

Record-Breaking Prize Pool

The 2026 iteration boasts the largest prize pool in the event's history, with a total of 30 million yen up for grabs across all titles. This doubles the previous year's offering, raising the stakes significantly for the global talent in attendance.

Debut Titles and Legacy Games

This year's iteration introduces several major titles to the Evo Japan competitive circuit for the first time. Attendees will compete in the highly anticipated team-based fighter 2XKO and SNK's newest entry, Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves. Additionally, Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O. World Stage makes its debut, signaling a resurgence for the 3D fighting game series.

Honoring the region's rich arcade history, the lineup also includes the legendary Hokuto no Ken (Fist of the North Star), alongside returning favorites like Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising, The King of Fighters XV, Melty Blood: Type Lumina, Under Night In-Birth II Sys:Celes, and Vampire Savior.

Registration

Registration and ticket sales are managed through the official Evo Japan website. Competitors can purchase Early Bird tickets for a limited time, with general admission passes available for spectators wishing to experience the event floor and vendor exhibits.

brussels-challenge-major-edition-2026.name: Brussels Challenge Major Edition 2026
brussels-challenge-major-edition-2026.description:
Brussels Challenge Major Edition 2026 is one of Europe's premier community-run fighting game tournaments, returning to Brussels, Belgium from May 8 through May 10, 2026. Organized by CCL, the event has grown from its roots as a single-day Street Fighter showcase in 2017 into a multi-game major that draws top competitors from across the European region and beyond.

For the FGC, Brussels Challenge Major is a fixture of the European spring circuit, known for its competitive depth, passionate crowd, and consistent ability to surface top regional talent alongside established international names.

Game Lineup

The 2026 edition features a strong multi-game slate anchored by Street Fighter 6, Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, and Samurai Shodown, with additional titles rounding out the weekend.

A Proven European Stage

Brussels Challenge has built a reputation as one of the most welcoming major events on the European calendar, combining a serious competitive environment with strong production and community atmosphere. Past editions have featured top European players across titles including Tekken 8, Guilty Gear Strive, The King of Fighters XV, and Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising.

Registration

Registration for Brussels Challenge Major Edition 2026 is handled through the event's start.gg page. Players can enter individual game brackets directly, with rules following standard double-elimination format.

road-to-ewc-dreamhack-atlanta.name: Road to EWC: DreamHack Atlanta
road-to-ewc-dreamhack-atlanta.location: 285 Andrew Young International Blvd NW, Atlanta, GA 30313
road-to-ewc-dreamhack-atlanta.description:
DreamHack Atlanta 2026 brings the world's largest gaming lifestyle festival back to the American South. Scheduled for May 15 through May 17, 2026, the event takes place at the Georgia World Congress Center in Atlanta, Georgia, serving as the festival's only U.S. stop of the year.

For the fighting game community, DreamHack Atlanta 2026 is a must-attend stop on the competitive calendar. With open brackets, high-stakes qualifiers, and a venue packed with thousands of fellow competitors and fans, the event delivers a full FGC weekend experience in the heart of Atlanta.

Road to EWC: FGC Qualifiers

The centerpiece of the FGC programming is the Road to EWC, a series of open qualifiers feeding directly into the Esports World Cup 2026. Competitors will battle for coveted EWC spots across three major fighting titles: Street Fighter 6, TEKKEN 8, and Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, making this one of the most consequential FGC stops of the 2026 season.

Beyond the Brackets

Outside of tournament competition, DreamHack Atlanta offers the full festival experience. Attendees can explore a massive expo floor, an Artist Alley, a cosplay championship, and a Creator Hub, with the DreamHack Stage hosting community programming and panels throughout the weekend.

Registration

Tickets for DreamHack Atlanta 2026 are available through the official DreamHack website via Tixr. General admission passes grant access to the full festival floor, expo, and spectator areas. Players competing in open tournaments, including the Road to EWC FGC qualifiers, can register through their respective event pages on the DreamHack Atlanta site.

3f-fight-for-the-future-2026.name: 3F Fight For the Future 2026
3f-fight-for-the-future-2026.location: Av. Cásper Líbero, 115 - Santa Ifigênia, São Paulo - SP, 01033-001, Brazil
3f-fight-for-the-future-2026.description:
3F Fight For the Future 2026 is Brazil's premier annual fighting game major, returning to São Paulo on May 16 and 17, 2026. Organized by Fighting Games Club SP, the event has established itself as the most important stop on the South American FGC calendar, combining fierce open bracket competition with a strong community atmosphere.

For Street Fighter 6 players, 3F is the top competitive opportunity in the region. Past editions have drawn the largest SF6 fields in South America, making it a critical proving ground for Brazilian and Latin American talent on the international circuit.

Brazil's Biggest FGC Stage

3F was born out of FGClub's ambition to host the largest fighting game event ever seen in Brazil, and the event has grown year over year since its debut. Alongside Street Fighter 6, the 2026 edition features an official Tekken World Tour stop and 2XKO, with additional titles rounding out the weekend.

Registration

Registration is handled through the official start.gg page. Advance registration is required as there are no on-site signups on event day. Competitor passes grant full venue access across both days, and all entry fees are converted directly into the tournament prize pot.

combo-breaker-2026.name: Combo Breaker 2026
combo-breaker-2026.location: 1551 Thoreau Dr N, Schaumburg, IL 60173, USA
combo-breaker-2026.description:
Combo Breaker 2026 returns to the Renaissance Schaumburg Convention Center for its annual Memorial Day Weekend celebration of fighting games. Scheduled for May 22–24, 2026, the event is one of the largest competitive gaming conventions in North America, featuring a 24-game official tournament lineup that includes Street Fighter 6, Tekken 8, 2XKO, and Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves.

Registration is managed via start.gg with an attendance cap of 8,500. The event is known for its 24/7 venue access, massive Artist Alley, and the 'All In Together' program, which allocates dedicated resources for community-run side tournaments. Early bird registration for competitors ($115) and spectators ($90) is available until February 1, 2026.

blink-respawn-2026.name: BLINK RESPAWN 2026
blink-respawn-2026.location: Sambil, Av. John F. Kennedy, Santo Domingo 10413, Dominican Republic
blink-respawn-2026.description:
Blink Respawn 2026 returns to the Dominican Republic as the Caribbean's premier esports and fighting game festival. Scheduled for June 5 through June 7, 2026, the event will take place at Sambil Santo Domingo, bringing together competitors, creators, and gaming communities from across LATAM, North America, and beyond.

As a major international destination, the 2026 tournament features a high-stakes lineup on an epic stage. The main event is headlined by a massive open bracket for Street Fighter 6, which proudly serves as an official Capcom Pro Tour Offline Premier Event, ensuring world-class talent will be in attendance to fight for the trophy.

Premier Tournament Lineup

Beyond Street Fighter 6, the competitive roster includes heavy-hitting staples and modern favorites. Attendees will clash in Super Smash Bros. Ultimate, Riot's tag-team fighter 2XKO, and SNK's latest acclaimed release, Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, which is featured as an SWC 2026 Official Tournament.

More Than a Tournament

Blink Respawn is designed to be a full destination experience fueled by energy, culture, and real competition. Attendees can take a break from the brackets to enjoy casual play zones, trading card game tournaments, free-play tables, and live community moments alongside top content creators.

Registration

Registration and bracket sign-ups are managed through Start.gg and the official Blink Respawn website. Competitors can secure their spots in the tournament until late registration closes on May 30, 2026. General admission passes are available online or at the venue for spectators wishing to experience the live commentators, the official Blink Respawn store, and the electrifying main stage.

hessen-crash-xxiii.name: Hessen Crash XXIII
hessen-crash-xxiii.location: Louisenstraße 58, 61348 Bad Homburg vor der Höhe, Germany
hessen-crash-xxiii.description:
Hessen Crash XXIII returns as Germany's longest-running fighting game community event, bringing the European scene together since 2010. Scheduled for June 5 through June 7, 2026, the event will take place inside the historic Kurhaus Bad Homburg, welcoming competitors of all skill levels.

As a cornerstone of the European fighting game community, the 2026 tournament features a highly competitive main lineup. The brackets will be headlined by massive showdowns in Street Fighter 6, Tekken 8, and Riot's tag-team fighter 2XKO, drawing top regional talent to battle for the championship titles.

Premier Tournaments and SWC Qualifiers

Beyond the core lineup, attendees will clash in Arc System Works favorites like Guilty Gear Strive and Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising. The event also proudly hosts an official SWC Qualifier for SNK's Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, adding international stakes to the weekend's competition.

Community and Side Brackets

Hessen Crash is celebrated for its dedication to the broader community and legacy titles. The weekend will feature dedicated side events, including brackets for BlazBlue: Central Fiction, Guilty Gear Xrd REV 2, and a special Lethal League Blaze tournament bolstered by a 500 Euro pot bonus.

Registration

Registration and bracket sign-ups are managed through Start.gg and the official Hessen Crash website. Competitors can secure Early Bird and Regular passes before late registration begins, with Weekend and Day passes available for spectators looking to experience the casual setups, community atmosphere, and high-level tournament streams.

the-mixup-2026-round-8.name: The MIXUP 2026 - Round 8
the-mixup-2026-round-8.location: Pl. de la Bourse, 69002 Lyon, France
the-mixup-2026-round-8.description:
The MIXUP 2026 - Round 8 returns to France as one of Europe's most prestigious fighting game tournaments. Scheduled for June 13 through June 14, 2026, the event will take place at Espace 140 in Lyon, bringing together top competitors and passionate fans from across the continent and the world.

As a crucial stop on multiple international circuits, the 2026 tournament features an incredibly high stakes lineup. The main stage will be headlined by massive brackets for TEKKEN 8, which serves as a highly coveted TWT Master Event and an Esports World Cup 2026 qualifier, ensuring that global heavyweights will clash for crucial circuit points.

Premier Circuit Qualifiers

The competitive stakes extend well beyond a single title. Attendees will also battle it out in SNK's Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves and The King of Fighters XV, both proudly featured as official Master Tier qualifiers for the SWC 2026. Additionally, Riot Games' tag team fighter 2XKO takes center stage as an official Challenger Tier event in the 2026 Competitive Series.

Community and Legacy Events

The MIXUP is deeply celebrated for its dedication to the vibrant French arcade community. The weekend will feature dedicated LaDOSETV classic arcade action, including brackets for Super Street Fighter II X, Street Fighter III: 3rd Strike, and Garou: Mark of the Wolves, alongside modern favorites like Guilty Gear Strive and Street Fighter 6.

Registration

Registration and bracket sign ups are managed through Start.gg and the official MIXUP website. Competitors can secure their passes before registration closes, with spectator tickets available for those looking to experience the intense matches, classic arcade setups, and the electrifying European crowd.
